title: Garlicky, Dense Basque Rice Paella
description: A hearty, flavorful Spanish dish from the Basque region, featuring savory garlic and a rich bain-marie of seasoned rice vinegar. This paella is perfect for adventurous eaters and flexitarian-friendly meals.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a paella pan or large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1-2 minutes, until fragrant.
Add the arborio rice and cook for 1-2 minutes, until lightly toasted.
Add the smoked paprika, crushed tomato, chicken broth, lemon juice, rice vinegar, salt, and black pepper. Stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the liquid has been absorbed and the rice is cooked.
If using, sprinkle the chopped fresh parsley on top of the paella and serve hot.
Cook the paella for an additional 10-15 minutes, until the top layer is crispy and golden brown.
Remove the paella from the heat and let it rest for 5 minutes before serving.
